subject: fix(ux): comprehensive mobile and UX improvements across 23 files
body:
Addresses iOS Safari issues, touch targets, accessibility, and general
UX clarity for non-technical users on mobile devices.

Mobile fixes:
- Prevent iOS auto-zoom on inputs (text-base on mobile, text-sm on desktop)
- Add viewport-fit=cover for iPhone notch/home indicator support
- Replace min-h-screen with min-h-dvh for iOS Safari dynamic toolbar
- Toast safe-area inset for iPhone home indicator
- Modal scrollable content with max-height for keyboard overlap
- Reduce modal padding on mobile (p-4 sm:p-6)
- Increase touch targets on move buttons, modal close, notification delete (44px min)
- Replace "Sign out" text with icon on mobile to reduce header crowding
- Reduce header gap to gap-1 on mobile
- Stack signature log IP/user-agent vertically on mobile
- Fix notification dropdown rubber-banding with overscroll-contain
- Add breadcrumb scroll fade indicator

UX clarity:
- Ready-to-sign toggle now says "Undo ready status" when active
- Back buttons styled as recognizable button with hover state
- Edit mode shows "Editing" badge for visual indicator
- Batch sign "Cancel" changed to "Done" for clearer intent
- "Counterparty" renamed to "Other party" in header role switcher
- Tooltip on role switcher explains counterparty meaning
- Truncated card titles now show full text on hover
- Version history shows last-updated timestamp on current version
- Expired modules: move/delete buttons disabled with explanatory tooltip

Accessibility:
- aria-describedby links form errors to their inputs
- aria-invalid on inputs with errors
- role="alert" on error messages
- aria-hidden on decorative SVGs in back buttons
- Module content viewer uses break-words for long content

Tags: ux, mobile, accessibility, ios, pwa
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: polish-hardening
Semver: patch
